description_long: This house features a sprawling, single-story layout with a complex, multi-gabled roof in dark gray shingles. The front facade is defined by a covered porch supported by columns, flanked by two prominent gabled sections with large windows. The exterior walls are finished in a light, neutral color, contrasting with the dark roof. Multiple windows of varying sizes are symmetrically arranged across the structure, providing ample natural light. The rear of the house includes a large, open gable with exposed beams and a covered patio area. Architectural details such as the chimney, porch overhangs, and dormer windows add to its traditional suburban character.
description: A large, single-story house with a complex dark gabled roof, light exterior walls, a covered front porch, and symmetrically arranged windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch with columns, two large gabled sections, and multiple windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the house, the extended rooflines, and a small side entry with its own overhang.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a large open gable with exposed beams, a covered patio area, and symmetrical window placement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended roof structure, additional windows, and the overall length of the house.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with large front-facing windows
attribute_entryway_design: Covered front porch with columns, steps, and a central door beneath a gabled overhang
